rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order Regarding Newly Acquired Medical Records Obtained After The Close Of Discovery; Request For Sanctions Against Berry Berry
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Plaintiffs¿ motion for protective order regarding newly acquired medical records and request for sanctions: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45a.m., not 9:00a.m. Both sides must bring proposed orders to the hearing. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT JAMES HANSON VS. SEQUOIA VENTURES, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Discovery Responses From Plaintiff
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Tuesday, July 2, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Frank M. Booth, Inc.'s motion to compel discovery responses from plaintiff is off calendar per moving party. = (503/TLJ)
